criteria.description: Verdict appears at the top of the report
criteria.check: The emitted audit report contains the string 'Verdict:' within its first 10 lines, before any acceptance-criteria table or findings section.

criteria.description: Every PASS criterion has a file:line citation
criteria.check: For every row in the acceptance-criteria table marked 'PASS', the Evidence column contains a citation matching the pattern '<filename>:<line-number>' (e.g. 'src/auth.js:42'). A PASS row with no such citation is a FAIL of this criterion.

criteria.description: Anti-pattern scan runs independently of acceptance criteria
criteria.check: The report contains a 'Findings' section that is populated by scanning the diff directly, not derived solely from the acceptance-criteria checklist. If the acceptance criteria all PASS but the diff contains a known anti-pattern category (test tampering, error handling rot, silenced signals, surface-only fixes, scope drift), the finding still appears in this section.

criteria.description: Acceptance criteria are derived from the original ask, not paraphrased
criteria.check: The acceptance-criteria checklist uses the user's own terms (or verbatim AC-## IDs from a spec file if one was provided). No criterion is stated in the implementer's narrative or purely in technical terms invented by Karen.
